title: Run Hermes inventory
description: Discover Hermes paths, state DB shape, cron availability, Obsidian notes, and runtime tools.

title: Build dashboard shell
description: Create Python server, local JSON data files, and first clean dark dashboard UI.

title: Begin Phase 3 Hermes search and config viewer
description: Add read-only message-level search over Hermes state.db and a masked Hermes config/model viewer in Settings.

title: Improve Phase 3 session reader and observed-agent analytics
description: Highlight message search terms, jump to the matched message inside a focused conversation window, and add historical session analytics without claiming live agent tracking.

title: Add reproducible dependency manifest for Agent OS
description: Create a requirements.txt or pyproject.toml that captures Agent OS runtime dependencies, especially Google Calendar integration packages, so setup is reproducible and not tied to Brandon's current machine.

title: Upgrade dashboard health from soft heartbeat to real subsystem health
description: Make /api/health report meaningful status for state.db readability, config readability, calendar auth or fallback state, cron/jobs availability, and degraded conditions instead of always reporting healthy.

title: Add behavior-oriented regression tests for key dashboard flows
description: Supplement string-contract tests with higher-value endpoint, data-shape, and interaction-focused checks for calendar payloads, attention resolution, project hydration, and other critical read-only flows.

title: Add safer local server startup and shutdown handling
description: Add a safer local Agent OS server lifecycle workflow with preflight port checks, runtime state tracking, and stop/status helpers so stale listeners and restart conflicts are easier to resolve while keeping the dashboard local-only.

title: Tighten Hermes launcher and Python listener lifecycle coupling
description: Narrow the remaining Windows dev-loop mismatch between Hermes-managed background sessions and the real Agent OS listener. Project-side mitigations now exist (bash-native launch/stop/status helpers plus launcher PID diagnostics in runtime state), but killing a Hermes background session still does not reliably terminate the Windows listener, so the remaining work is now a Hermes/process-manager integration follow-up.

title: Reduce monolith risk in server.py and app.js without changing scope
description: Completed the first scoped light modularization slice: runtime config parsing moved to runtime_config.py, shared browser constants/markdown/API helpers moved to public/core.js, index.html loads core.js before app.js, visible dashboard self-reference copy and startup output use Mentat, and verification now covers the split with 36/36 tests.

title: Choose the next small Mentat modularization slice
description: Continue modularization only as needed, one concern at a time. Recommended candidates are extracting health checks, Hermes/session data access, project/task/attention data helpers, or one frontend view renderer. Keep endpoint contracts stable and do not introduce React/build tooling yet.

title: Build runtime config foundation and audit hardcoded local assumptions
description: Add a layered Agent OS runtime config system (shared TOML defaults, local override TOML, env vars, and CLI flags), move key local path assumptions out of code, and document which Brandon-specific defaults remain intentional.

title: Design Agent Pulse 2.0 heartbeat model
description: Turn Agent Pulse from the current read-only historical session count into an active Phase 4 heartbeat/status panel backed by project-owned write-back data: running/idle/blocked/done/failed state, current task, last heartbeat, latest output, and needs-user-input flag.

title: Connect Google Calendar later
description: After the dashboard shell is stable, connect read-only Google Calendar integration via OAuth.

title: Finish Phase 2.5 UI/UX stabilization review
description: Review Today, Projects, and Tasks desktop/mobile UX after the command-center redesign; refine the connected header/task rail, project portfolio rail, dropdown behavior, screenshots, and any remaining redundancy before larger integrations.

title: Polish Google Calendar read-only integration
description: Turn the connected Google Calendar source into a useful Today/Calendar experience: today and 7-day agenda, fallback/stale states, event grouping, and read-only OAuth boundaries.

title: Build dashboard-native project and task creation
description: Add local-only create/edit flows for projects and tasks backed by project-owned data files, with validation, safe write-back endpoints, and no Hermes core mutation.

title: Evaluate Kanban or richer task board write-back
description: After the grouped list model settles, decide whether to add Kanban or a richer task board backed by project-owned JSON/API instead of Hermes core files.

title: Add read-only email pane after calendar stabilizes
description: Design a safe email panel for priority messages and needs-attention surfacing after calendar is stable; keep it read-only until Brandon explicitly approves send/delete actions.

title: Add Windows startup service docs and safe remote access option
description: Document running Agent OS on Windows login and evaluate Tailscale/VPN-only remote access; do not expose port 8888 publicly.

title: Reassess React migration only when interactions justify it
description: Keep the dashboard vanilla JS during UI stabilization; revisit React if direct editing, modals, routing, live agent roster, or websocket updates make the frontend too interaction-heavy.

title: Run Wilson Milsen review before the next major expansion
description: Before adding larger integrations, ask Wilson Milsen for a read-only YAGNI/code review of AGENTS.md, README.md, server.py, frontend files, data JSON, and the current diff; implement only high-impact safe suggestions.

title: Complete full Agent OS to Mentat rename boundary
description: Postpone the full repo/module/data/docs rename until the codebase is stable and a compatibility plan is chosen. The current recommendation is to keep the user-facing brand as Mentat now, preserve Agent OS / agent-os internally for compatibility, and do the larger rename as a deliberate later milestone with aliases/migration notes.
